Ad Text:
About Us 

At Lindt ‘We Enchant the World with Chocolate’ and this is an amazing opportunity for the right individual to join us and become part of our high performing team.

Our rich and diverse culture embraces change while cherishing our history. At Lindt, we believe that excellence lies within each and every one of our employees.

That's why we've created a one-of-a-kind environment where our team members can take risks, challenge the norm, and help build the Lindt brand for generations to come.

With teams of passionate employees, we're always on the lookout for fresh ideas and innovative thinking. We encourage our team to recognise opportunities, get creative, and build strong relationships to Enchant the World with Chocolate.

About the Role 

We are looking for a highly skilled person to drive the commercialisation of the ANZ Category Growth Strategy by developing and executing commercial outcomes that drive category growth. The role will lead the category conversation with key customers in the chocolate confectionery market, applying deep category and shopper insight to evolving market dynamics, enabling customer‑centric solutions and delivering sustainable premium growth.

About You

You have the expertise at a senior category level and able to turn insights into action.
Demonstrated experience working with retailers and delivering positive outcomes.
Able to develop and execute strategic initiatives at the customer and channel level.
Exceptional analytical skills, with the ability to prioritise decisions for solutions across business processes.
High levels of computer literacy, and proficiency with Circana, Quantium and Advanced Excel
Extensive experience producing clear and concise reports to a broad group of stakeholders.
Exceptional organisational skills and attention to detail.
Strong verbal and written communication skills.
The ability to represent & lead the category/shopper perspective in a dynamic and fast paced cross functional working environment.



If you are passionate about driving business outcomes and contributing positively to change, whilst building strong relationships with stakeholders apply now.

About the role 
An exciting, business-critical role with a multinational FMCG organisation currently exists in order to fill a key role in their Finance team. This Senior Financial Analyst (FP&A) position will provide financial support and results analysis and will drive accountability for the financial commitments of the business. The continued development and enhancement of reporting processes, in addition to discovering data insights by utilising advanced business intelligence tools such as Tableau, are a key focus of this role. Initially, this role will commence as a long-term 12 month fixed-term contract, however there is significant scope for extension and/or permanency.

The responsibilities
Reporting to the Group FP&A Manager, you will be responsible for:

Work with internal stakeholders to develop and design business requirements regarding data and information management
Partner with cross-functional teams in developing progressive data modelling & visualisation platforms, enabling discovery and interpretation of data insights to further unlock business growth opportunities
Create interactive Tableau Dashboards that incorporate good visual design principles
Documentation and improvement of reporting/dashboard processes
Preparation of Reporting Packs (Month end, forecast S&OP cycles, budget)
Understanding of key cost drivers, revenue drivers and variances versus plan/forecast
End-to-End process management of forecast and budget compilation including stakeholder communication
Consolidation of various categories and updating of reporting systems and reports
The successful applicant will have 
Ideally, you will be a CA/CPA qualified accounting professional and will possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills as your ability to interact and engage cross-functionally within the business will be crucial to your success. Additionally, you will have 1.5-2yrs of hands-on Tableau experience, have worked in a large, complex organisation previously and feel comfortable working with large data sets.

Additional benefits

Partial flexible working arrangements (2 days WFH)
Dynamic company culture
Excellent mentorship from senior management
Substantial salary package
About the client 
Our client is an instantly recognizable name residing within the FMCG sector. With a diverse portfolio of well-known household brands, they have now been recognized as an "employer of choice" in the market.

How to apply 
To apply online, please click on the appropriate link below. Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Jamie Burgess on 0439 014 028 or jburgess@sharpandcarter.com.au, quoting Ref No JB2443.

Category:
Analysis & Reporting (Banking & Financial Services)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Summary

This role as the Strategic Program Reporting Analyst gives you the opportunity to care about impact. You enjoy designing fit-for-purpose analytics frameworks, improving ways of working, and seeing insight directly inform strategic decisions.

If you’re looking for a role where your analysis genuinely shapes strategic direction — and where clarity and integrity matter — this is an opportunity to step into driving enterprise strategy and program governance.

The Organisation

Airservices Australia is a government-owned organisation responsible for the safe and efficient management of 11% of the world’s airspace and provision of aviation rescue fire fighting services at Australia’s busiest airports.

Our people are our greatest asset with a dynamic and diverse team operating from locations across the country - from bustling cities to regional and remote locations, including an island. This team keep are helping to secure the future of Australia’s skies and keep it amongst the safest in the world.

We connect people with their world safely – linking family and friends, generating economic activity, creating jobs, and facilitating trade and tourism.

Airservices is committed to fostering a culture that is diverse, inclusive, and respectful.  We encourage motivated individuals who love what they do, value a service first mindset and embrace a challenge to explore a career with Airservices. In return you will be a valued team member, be offered flexibility and experience a meaningful career in an exciting, ever-evolving aviation industry.

Our Leaders

Leaders at Airservices demonstrate our Leadership Standard in the way they Know, Focus and Value our people every day. Our leaders role model our values of Safe always, Service first, Love what we do, Work as one, Own it to connect people with their world safely.

An Airservices leader leads inclusively, thinks strategically, collaborates and communicates with impact to deliver outcomes that support our culture of trust, care and accountability.

The Role & The Team

Reporting into the Strategic Pipeline Lead, the Reporting Analyst sits within the Strategic Financial Management function — one of four pillars driving long-term financial sustainability. The team exists to bring clarity, confidence and consistency to how strategic financial initiatives are tracked, measured and assured across the organisation.

In this highly influential role, you will shape how insights are generated, told and trusted. You’ll lead thinking across the finance division on analytics and assurance, design frameworks that enable better decisions, and provide transparent, objective reporting that underpins strategic outcomes.

Please see the link below to view the full Position Description:

 PD Final Senior Reporting Analyst.docx

The Person

You are a sharp, commercially minded analyst who thrives on turning complex data into clear, compelling insight. You’re curious, strategic and confident influencing across a diverse stakeholder group.

You’re comfortable challenging assumptions, identifying gaps and providing unbiased assurance, all while collaborating constructively with program and business leaders.

To be successful in this role, you bring:

A tertiary or recognised qualification in financial analysis, portfolio management, planning and analysis, or a related field
Proven experience delivering trusted reporting, analytics and insight in senior or enterprise-level environments
A strong background in finance, commercial, transformation or delivery-focused teams
The ability to translate complex data into meaningful insights and actionable outcomes
Well-developed consulting, problem-solving and stakeholder engagement skills
Confidence building strong partnerships across diverse professional and organisational settings
Experience within portfolio, program or project environments is highly regarded
Exposure to both public and private sectors is desirable

At Airservices Australia, we embrace and thrive on diversity of thought, perspectives, and capabilities because we know the benefits of having a workforce that is representative of the community that we serve. We appreciate everyone is unique, and acknowledge diversity in thought leads to greater transparency and improved consideration in our decision making.

We believe a diverse workforce supported by an inclusive culture is central to our success. We actively encourage and welcome candidates from all cultural backgrounds, gender identities, people living with disability and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples to apply.

Security Clearance 

To be eligible to apply, you must be an Australian Citizen or a Permanent resident as an Airservices Background check is required for this role. Please refer to the security check requirements successful applicants will be required to undertake as part of the recruitment process.

Should you require any further information please contact TA Partner, Christine Collins at christine.collins@airservicesaustralia.com

How to Apply

To apply for this position please lodge an application online before Applications close 11.55 pm (AEST) Tuesday 07 April 2026.

#ASA2

We currently have an opportunity for a Senior Business Improvement Analyst to join our Government & Integrated Facilities Management team and play a key role in driving operational excellence across our contract portfolio through data‑driven insights, process optimisation, and governance uplift. Reporting to the Analyst Team Lead, you'll help deliver high‑quality outcomes that support our essential services and enable sustainable performance for our clients and communities.



What You'll Do

This role offers a broad scope, with key responsibilities including:


Analysing operational data and performance metrics to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement across the portfolio.

Leading continuous improvement initiatives that enhance governance, standardisation, compliance, and delivery efficiency.

Developing insights and recommendations that support sustainable financial performance and an improved customer experience.

Partnering with operational, finance, and leadership teams to embed best‑practice frameworks and drive meaningful change.

Designing, maintaining, and evolving Power BI dashboards and reporting frameworks to support informed decision‑making.




What You'll Bring


Proven experience in business improvement, data analysis, or reporting roles, with a strong focus on outcomes and impact.

Advanced capability in reporting and visualisation tools such as Power BI and Excel, with working knowledge of SQL or similar query languages.

Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with the confidence to translate complex data into clear, practical insights.

Collaborative stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence and support teams through change.

A positive, growth‑oriented mindset and a genuine commitment to continuous improvement and shared success.




More than anything, you'll bring a strong work ethic, a positive attitude, and a commitment to delivering high-quality outcomes for our clients.



Why Downer?

At Downer, we plan, create, and sustain essential public services across Australia and New Zealand. From hospitals to data centres and defence partnerships, we manage critical infrastructure that keeps communities thriving.



What It Means to Belong at Downer

We're proud of our high-performance culture and commitment to diversity, offering:


Training and development to support and diversify your career.

With 30% of vacancies filled internally you'll find real opportunities to advance your career.

Perks@Downer: healthcare discounts, great savings on retail purchases, car purchases and much more.

Employee Assistance Program: professional support for you and your family when you need it most.




You want your work to matter and so do we. With over 26,000 people across Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific, our team is made up of individuals with unique perspectives, backgrounds, and ideas.

We know that diversity makes us stronger and we actively celebrate it through our commitment to inclusion and belonging.



We're committed to building a team that reflects the diverse communities we serve and we welcome people of all ages, genders, sexual orientations, cultures, abilities, and lived experiences. We especially encourage applications from those whose voices have traditionally been underrepresented in our industry, including women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, Māori and Pasifika Peoples, veterans, people with disability, and neurodivergent individuals. Even if your experience doesn't align perfectly with this role, we'd still like to hear from you. If it feels like the right fit, apply - potential counts, and so do you.



As a WORK180 Endorsed Employer, we support flexibility that works for your life, inclusive leadership that values your voice, and equitable access to opportunity so you can do your best work and bring your whole self to it.



If you need support or adjustments through the recruitment process, just let us know, we're here to help you put your best foot forward.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

About the team

Our Finance team are a vital partner in every aspect of our business, providing commercial insights to drive our success and influence everything we do. With presence in every department, we analyse performance, and deliver actionable insights that shape our strategic decisions.

About the role


As Finance Business Partner - Services, you will be a trusted partner to leaders, providing clear insights, disciplined cost management and commercial analysis that drives performance. You will lead forecasting and planning cycles, develop robust financial models, and translate complex information into actionable recommendations for operational leaders. Your key responsibilities will be:

Produce accurate, timely reporting that clearly connects operational activity with financial outcomes
Analyse cost trends, variances and operational behaviours to identify improvement opportunities
Build and maintain driver‑based budgets and forecasts that reflect operational reality
Prepare executive‑ready materials for performance discussions and leadership forums
Build strong, trust‑based relationships across Services, Procurement, Store Operations and Finance
Translate complex financial insights into clear messages that support confident decision‑making
Support cost‑out and productivity initiatives by modelling benefits and validating assumptions
Build compelling business cases with clear ROI, payback and scenario modelling
Leverage tools such as Power BI and automation solutions to improve insight delivery

About you and your skills


You bring strong commercial finance capability, excellent stakeholder engagement skills, and the ability to simplify complexity. You enjoy partnering with operational leaders and influencing decision-making through clear insights and analysis. You will bring:



Proven experience in commercial finance or finance business partnering with a large and complex environment (retail/FMCG preferred).
CA, CPA, or similar business qualification preferred.
Business partnering experience in retail or FMCG desirable but not essential.
Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills, with the ability to build effective relationships.
Clear and compelling storytelling skills to communicate insights effectively.
Proven ability to prepare, interpret, and analyse financial information to identify opportunities, risks, and recommended actions.
Adaptable and flexible working style, responsive to changing business needs.
Strong collaboration skills, contributing effectively within team environments.
Highly motivated and outcome‑focused, with a track record of driving initiatives to delivery.

What’s in it for you?

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

About the recruitment process

We’re continuing to build a gender equitable team, and a culture that is just as diverse, inclusive and welcoming as the communities we serve. We are committed to creating a workplace that is safe and respectful for our team. We encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures (including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples), abilities, sexual orientation and gender identities.

We’re happy to adjust our recruitment process to support candidates with disability. For further information and additional contact details visit the ‘Our Recruitment Process’ section of our careers site or email inclusionrecruitment@coles.com.au

Turn data into decisions — deliver powerful process insights that drive simplification, efficiency and real business value!
Lead enterprise diagnostics and solution design, challenging legacy processes and shaping future-state ways of working!
Partner with senior stakeholders to enable transformation, improving outcomes for customers, colleagues and the business!

Our people are customer obsessed. They prioritise the needs and satisfaction of the customer above all else.  Our mindset fosters innovation and creates strong, lasting customer relationships as we strive to be the most customer centric company in Australia and New Zealand.

In this Senior Consultant Diagnostics and Solutions role, you will be joining the Enterprise Simplification team that’s doubling down on keeping it simple.

Each day, you’ll go above and beyond to:

Be a seasoned expert in lean six sigma, process reengineering, and business improvement.
With a focus on customer experience and business performance, lead diagnostics to solve complex enterprise-wide business problems and recommend how we simplify our end-to-end processes and propositions for our must win battles and technology modernisation.
Baseline and analyse current state, identify inefficiencies, customer experience opportunities and reimagine the future state.
Design, test and simulate future-state solutions, assessing impacts, benefits and alignment with strategic objectives.
Act as a thought leader in diagnostics and solution design, contributing to standards, methods, and continuous improvement of enterprise capabilities.
Engage, influence, and collaborate with senior stakeholders, translating complex insights into clear recommendations and actionable outcomes.

We’re looking for the best and brightest to deliver the best for our customers. You’ll need:

A Lean Six Sigma Black Belt with process mining experience highly regarded.
Demonstrated ability to lead large scale enterprise problems and you have a history of tangible benefit outcomes.
A passion for using data to make the problem clear. You will have a distinct skill at synthesising complex information, so it makes sense to stakeholders.
Strong cognitive skills, great with numbers, and have strong business acumen. 
You will have great influencing skills, resilience, and you are comfortable with ambiguity.
Financial services, service industries, or consulting experience preferred.

A diverse and inclusive workplace works better for everyone 

We know that our people make us who we are. That's why we have built a culture of equity and respect – where everyone feels valued and appreciated for being their authentic selves. In partnership with our multiple Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) we continue to foster an inclusive environment, where all NAB colleagues’ unique backgrounds and identities are understood, respected and celebrated.  We are committed to providing an environment where you can work your way.

Lead advanced process mining and analytics, identifying root causes, simulating future states and challenging legacy ways of working!
Partner with senior stakeholders to enable transformation, building process mining capability and improving outcomes for customers and colleagues at NAB!

Process mining delivery experience essential.
Strong analytical and critical thinking capabilities.
Demonstrated ability to solve complex problems and create innovative/pragmatic opportunities to improve.
Ability to synthesize complex process information clearly for stakeholders.
Hands on track record facilitating process mining analysis for diagnostics, process efficiency, reengineering, and lean operations.
Demonstrated capability in facilitating data transformation for process mining.
Proven ability to lead cross-functional problem solving through ambiguity, delivering significant value with strong business acumen.
Build and maintain trusting relationships across an enterprise to ensure alignment and collaboration.

As the Group AI Manager, you will play a pivotal role in translating Service Stream’s enterprise AI strategy into real business outcomes. This leadership position focuses on driving transformation, uplifting organisational capability, and enabling enterprise-wide adoption of AI solutions. You will oversee the end‑to‑end delivery of AI initiatives - from identifying high‑value opportunities and building business cases, through to execution, value realisation, and managing change across the organisation.

This is a full-time permanent position, based out of our Collins Street office supporting hybrid working arrangements (3 days in office) and is only open to Australian Citizens.

About the role

• Serve as the primary point of contact for AI across the Group, advocating for AI adoption and enabling teams through training, guidance, and capability uplift.
• Own and manage the Group AI roadmap, ensuring initiatives are prioritised, sequenced, and aligned to strategic transformation goals.
• Lead development of robust business cases, defining business problems, recommended AI solutions, and projected commercial returns (ROI).
• Conduct cross-functional discovery workshops to identify AI opportunities and gather requirements for transformation initiatives.
• Establish and track KPIs to measure commercial value, ensuring delivery outcomes meet original business case expectations.
• Enforce Data & AI governance, ethical standards, and security controls across all AI initiatives.
• Provide executive-level reporting on roadmap progress, risks, and delivery performance.
• Oversee the full delivery lifecycle, coordinating internal teams and external partners to ensure quality outcomes.
• Manage third‑party suppliers and strategic partners, ensuring contractual performance and value delivery.
• Champion change adoption, ensuring AI solutions are embedded into business processes and operationalised effectively.

Skills and experience

•Must be an Australian Citizen.

• Significant experience in Data or Digital Transformation, Product Management, or similar transformation and delivery‑focused leadership roles in complex corporate environments.
• Strong stakeholder management and the ability to influence senior management, facilitate workshops, and drive cross‑functional alignment.
• Strong understanding of AI, Machine Learning, and Data Science concepts, with the ability to translate technical capability into business value.
• Proven track record of delivering measurable commercial outcomes through transformation initiatives.
• Demonstrated experience developing high‑quality business cases and investment proposals.
• Experience working within established governance, compliance, and risk frameworks.
• Strong change management capability, with experience driving adoption of new technologies or workflows.
• Excellent communication, analytical thinking, and problem‑solving skills.
• Ability to work collaboratively across diverse business units while also operating independently when required.

Qualifications

• Undergraduate degree in Business, Commerce, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field (mandatory).
• Certifications in Product Management (CPM, CSPO) and/or Project/Program Management (PRINCE2, PMP, Agile/Scrum) — highly regarded.
• Strong literacy in AI and data concepts, supported by relevant training or professional development.

About Us

Service Stream is an equal opportunity ASX‑listed business that develops and operates Australia’s essential service networks across telecommunications, utilities, transport, defence, and social infrastructure industries. We keep communities connected.

Benefits of working with us include:

• Flexible working – We genuinely consider all flexibility requests.
• Growth – Career development opportunities across our expanding business.
• Discounts – Access to hundreds of major retailers including Woolworths, Airbnb, JB Hi‑Fi, and Bupa.
• Paid leave – Parental, cultural, community service, study, corporate volunteering, and purchased leave.
• Culture – A workplace that values diversity, inclusion, and celebrates excellence.

How to apply

Please submit your interest via the Apply button, where you will be directed to create a profile in our system.

We celebrate diversity at Service Stream and welcome applications from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disabilities or neurodivergence, and those from LGBTIQA+, Veteran, or other diverse groups.

Applications will not be accepted via email.
If you require reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact: talent.corporate@servicestream.com.au

Job Title: Senior Project Manager (Finance Transformation) - Up to $1100/day - 12 months
Ad Text:
My client, one of Australia's leading Wealth Management companies, is currently seeking an experienced Senior Project Manager to join them for an initial 12 month daily rate contract, with the view for long term extension. The role will be responsible for supporting a large scale enterprise wide Finance Transformation, leading end-to-end initiatives across regulatory reporting, Foundation Data Model design & implementation, and project delivery. 

The benefits: 

·      12 month day rate contract with strong possibility for extension
·      ASAP start
·      Hybrid working arrangements - Melbourne offices
·      Supportive culture with focus on training & development
·      Strong opportunities for progression into different projects
·      Access Contractor Benefits Program: Enjoy discounts at hundreds of retail outlets, department stores, activities, novated lease, travel and restaurants nationwide
·      Get gym membership discounts with our partners
·      Enjoy Health Insurance discounts off all NIB retail health insurance products
·      Access EAP for wellbeing and mental health support

The requirements:
      
·      Strong Project Management experience in similar large-scale, complex transformation with multiple streams
·     Strong experience in regulatory reporting for the Superannuation industry
·     Strong Wealth domain experience (e.g. superannuation, investments)
·      Comfortable leading ambiguous, multi-stream programs of work
·      Strong program change  experience (Not just BAU)
·      Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
·      Strong experience in JIRA & Confluence
·      Project Management certifications highly advantageous

If you have the right skillset, please get in contact with Cat or send me your resume to cat.john@halcyonknights.com.au

#SCR-catrin-john

Job Title: Head of Technology - Data Enablement and MarTech Activation
Ad Text:
At Latitude, we make it possible – for our customers, our communities, and our people. We believe in creating opportunities that truly matter, helping you thrive both professionally and personally. That’s why we offer benefits that make a real difference in your life.




We make it possible…

About the role

The Head of Technology – Data Enablement & MarTech Activation leads the technology capabilities that make trusted data usable at scale and turn that data into measurable customer activation.




This role is accountable for an integrated strategy and delivery roadmap spanning modern data platforms and pipelines, enterprise data products, data management and quality, and the marketing technology stack that enables compliant, timely and optimised campaign execution.




You’ll build strong partnerships across Product, Risk / Compliance, Customer Care, and lead a high‑performing team that delivers real commercial value.




Responsibilities:

Own and execute an integrated roadmap across data enablement and marketing activation, ensuring clear prioritisation, governance and delivery transparency.

Lead a lean, hands‑on engineering capability to design and deliver scalable, secure, cloud‑native data platforms and pipelines.

Drive engineering excellence through CI/CD, automated testing, observability and DevSecOps practices across data and martech platforms.

Establish and maintain platform ownership disciplines including funding models, total cost of ownership, lifecycle management and vendor relationships.

Set direction for enterprise data products, defining outcomes, success measures and prioritised backlogs aligned to business needs.

Own the technology enablement of marketing activation, ensuring martech platforms are stable, integrated and optimised for compliant campaign execution.

Enable reliable, repeatable campaign delivery through strong release hygiene, testing, experimentation and performance optimisation.

Lead, develop and retain a multidisciplinary team, building a culture of ownership, continuous improvement and strong delivery discipline.

Manage operational costs and investments, and clearly articulate value delivered including ROI, revenue and cost benefits.




What you’ll bring:

Proven people leadership experience building and leading high‑performing multidisciplinary teams.

Strong strategic thinking and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to translate strategy into executable roadmaps.

Deep understanding of modern data platforms and engineering practices (e.g. cloud‑native architectures, pipelines, CI/CD, security‑by‑design).

Working knowledge of marketing technology and activation concepts, including marketing automation, CDP‑style data flows, personalisation and measurement.

Commercial and financial discipline, including experience managing cost, investment and vendor relationships.

An AI‑native mindset — regularly leveraging AI tools to enhance decision‑making, accelerate delivery and improve team efficiency.




Sometimes the best candidates don’t have 100% of what is listed above, but if you have most and are confident, you’d be a good fit, we’d love to hear from you!

We always support a safe, healthy, engaging, and productive working environment for all employees and workers, whether that be in your home and office, or a combination of both.

Digital payments, cards, loans are what we offer - but what we really are is a platform that helps people shop and live better. Always living and breathing our values. About Us
When people talk about the Flying Doctor, thoughts generally turn to red-bellied planes undertaking rescue missions in the Outback. However, the current Victorian story is one that reflects today's challenges for people living in remote and rural communities across the state. While we do still transport people by air and by road, this work is complemented by a range of primary health services that address the most common issues people living in country Victoria face when accessing health care.

About the Role
RFDS Victoria is seeking an experienced, strategic leader to shape and deliver our organisation-wide data, insights and digital enablement agenda. In this senior role, you will treat data as a high-value organisational asset—establishing a trusted “single source of truth”, uplifting data literacy, and enabling evidence-based decision-making that strengthens service delivery and long-term mission impact.

Key responsibilities include but not limited to:
- Leading the development and execution of an organisation‑wide Data, Insights and Digital Enablement Strategy that strengthens performance, service delivery and long‑term mission impact.
- Providing enterprise leadership for data governance, privacy, security and regulatory compliance, embedding robust frameworks and clear accountability for ethical, high‑quality data use.
- Overseeing the design, maturity and performance of enterprise analytics, forecasting, segmentation and AI‑enabled insight capabilities that inform strategic decision‑making.
- Setting strategic direction for enterprise data architecture, integration, cloud platforms and digital ecosystems to ensure scalability, security and future readiness.
- Driving organisation‑wide optimisation, automation, AI enablement and digital capability uplift in partnership with transformation and operational leaders.
- Leading high‑performing teams and major initiatives that align people, processes and technology, while monitoring performance, risks and value realisation across data and digital programs.

To be successful, you will need:
- Tertiary qualification in Data Science, Information Systems, Computer Science, Business Digital Transformation, Analytics, or a related discipline, demonstrating advanced strategic and technical expertise.
- Minimum of ten (10) years’ senior leadership experience delivering complex, organisation wide data, insights, digital transformation, or technology enabled change initiatives within a not for profit, health, government, or similarly complex, mission driven environment.
- Demonstrated leadership in enterprise data governance, architecture, and integration, including cloud platforms, data quality, privacy, security, and regulatory compliance.
- Extensive experience building and maturing enterprise analytics and digital ecosystems, spanning BI strategy, advanced analytics, AI/ML, CRM platforms, and omni‑channel engagement.
- Proven ability to translate complex data into strategic intelligence that drives executive decision‑making, organisational performance, and evidence‑based planning.
- Strong capability in aligning digital and data investments with organisational strategy, revenue growth, supporter value, and effective vendor and contract management.
- Satisfactory completion of safety screening including a current Victorian Drivers Licence, National Police Check, and the right to work in Australia.

What working at RFDS can offer you:
- Five non-accruable Office Closure Leave days each year
- Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
- Access to salary packaging options to reduce your taxable income
- Fitness passport – a discounted gym membership to gyms and pools that both you and your family can enjoy
- Access to discounted private health insurance
- Enjoy a full-time role with flexible work arrangements to work from home and at our Richmond office, as well as options for purchased leave.
- Great parental leave beneﬁts in addition to government parental leave.

About the role


As the Finance Business Partner - Liquor, you'll lead weekly trade analysis, support strategic initiatives, and drive financial governance across planning, forecasting, and supplier income. Your key responsibilities will include:


Delivering insights into key trade & margin drivers
Proactively seeks to identify risk and opportunities on sales and margin, and supports and influences to mitigate risks and drive materialisation of opportunities
Influence business decision making with fact based insightful and innovative analysis
Ensure plans are in place to deliver the strategic objectives of the Business Unit & strategic initiatives
Drive insight and ROI analysis of investment business cases such as range reviews
Track key initiatives and promotions to ensure they are on target to delivered expected outcome
Supporting annual strategic plan and budget process
Driving the quarterly forecast process ensuring all risks and opportunities are understood and communicated
Identifying and implementing process and system improvements to enhance performance and delivery against objectives


About you and your skill

You’re a qualified finance professional with strong leadership, retail/FMCG experience, and a passion for influencing outcomes. Your analytical skills and ability to communicate complex insights make you a trusted business partner. You'll also have:



Ideally CA, CPA or similar business qualification.
Business partnering experience in a retail/FMCG environment desirable but not essential.
Strong interpersonal and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence and build effective relationships.
Strong storytelling skills, with the ability to present insights clearly and effectively.
Proven experience in preparing, understanding and analysing financial information to identify business opportunities/challenges and recommend alternate actions.
Proven ability to prepare, interpret, and analyse financial information to identify opportunities and risks, and recommend actionable outcomes.
Adaptable and flexible working style, with the ability to respond effectively to changing stakeholder needs.
Strong collaboration skills, with experience working as part of a team, sharing knowledge, and contributing to overall team performance.
Highly motivated and outcome-focused, with a track record of driving initiatives through to completion and engaging stakeholders to ensure delivery.
Strong time management and organisational skills, with the ability to prioritise effectively and continuously improve processes.

What’s in it for you?

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.

Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.

Ad Text:
In this role, you will:
• Design, implement, and maintain a comprehensive reporting and governance framework to support the oversight of Brisbane 2032 Games delivery and legacy outcomes.
• Develop high-quality reports, briefings, and dashboards for senior executives, governance groups, and stakeholders, leveraging digital tools and data analytics to ensure clarity and precision.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams within OPGO and across local, state, and federal government agencies, as well as the Brisbane 2032 Organising Committee (OCOG) and other key Games Delivery Partners, to ensure alignment and consistency in reporting and governance processes.
• Lead the identification and management of risks, issues, and opportunities related to governance and reporting, ensuring alignment with government standards and best practices.
• Establish and maintain strong, collaborative relationships with internal and external stakeholders to foster a culture of accountability, transparency, and continuous improvement.

• Contribute to the successful delivery of the Brisbane 2032 Games by ensuring all reporting and governance activities align with the overarching Games vision and objectives.
• Contribute to a workplace culture based on values and behaviours, ensuring a positive work environment that is safe, healthy, diverse, respectful and inclusive.

This role offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to one of the most significant events in Queensland's history, shaping the governance and reporting frameworks that will underpin the success of the Brisbane 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games.

About Us


Want to be part of a high-performing, award-winning team? Look no further than UniSuper. We’re recognised for our performance excellence as we strive to deliver exceptional retirements for our members, and our growth means opportunities to grow with us. Come be a part of our inclusive, passionate team and make a difference with UniSuper.
Over 70% of all funds under management (FUM) are managed in-house by our dedicated Investments team. We strongly believe our in-house management strategy ultimately leads to greater retirement outcomes for our members through lower fees, sustainable investment options and top quartile investment performance.
This is an opportunity to support the implementation of UniSuper’s Private Markets strategy by contributing to high‑quality investment analysis and long‑term value creation for members.
 

The role


As an Investment Analyst, Private Markets, you will support the origination, execution and ongoing management of direct and fund investments across the portfolio. You will work closely with the Private Markets team to deliver robust analysis, reporting and insights.

Develop and maintain financial models for direct private market investments
Assist with due diligence for acquisitions and divestments, including financial and valuation analysis
Support active asset and portfolio management, performance monitoring and reporting
Prepare investment recommendations and materials for internal committees and stakeholders
Generate and present investment ideas and research across relevant sectors
Collaborate with other asset class teams within Investments

About you


You are an experienced investment analyst with a strong foundation in private markets investing and financial analysis. You bring a proactive, detail‑oriented approach and communicate clearly with a broad range of stakeholders.

3–5 years’ experience in private markets, funds management, investment banking or professional services
Strong financial modelling capability and ability to synthesise commercial insights
Excellent written and verbal communication and presentation skills
Highly analytical with strong quantitative and technology skills
Self‑motivated, detail‑focused and able to work autonomously and in teams
Relevant tertiary and professional qualifications (e.g. CA or CFA)

What We Offer


UniSuper believes that the best way to achieve great things is when we come together and collaborate. Therefore, we ask you to be able to commit to 60% of your time in office. UniSuper is proud of our culture and benefits, which empower our people to achieve their full potential, thrive, and grow their career with us. These include:


• Development opportunities and learning support
• Flexible working and ways to flex
• Competitive total rewards and benefits
 

It should go without saying, but at UniSuper, we value and celebrate diversity and inclusion. We believe that a variety of perspectives, backgrounds, interests, abilities, and skills is crucial for delivering great retirement outcomes for our members. We invite you to apply for the roles that suit your career aspirations, even if you don’t meet all the requirements.

About the role
We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support the delivery of complex transformation and regulatory & compliance initiatives across our organisation. This role sits within the Transformation & Technology function and works closely with Product Owners, Delivery Leads and senior stakeholders to define, plan and deliver sustainable business outcomes.
  
The Senior Business Analyst will lead analysis activities across large and high-risk initiatives, often operating in environments with regulatory, compliance and operational complexity. This role requires strong planning capability, confidence engaging at executive level, and the ability to work effectively across both Agile and Waterfall delivery models.
  
Working as part of an inclusive and collaborative team your key responsibilities will include:
  

Lead end-to-end business analysis activities across complex initiatives
Operate effectively within Agile and Waterfall delivery environments
Plan and manage scope, dependencies, risks and deliverables
Engage and influence senior and executive stakeholders, including clear and concise written communication
Translate regulatory and compliance requirements into practical business and system solutions
Define and document business requirements, user stories and process models
Lead and coordinate testing activities, including UAT planning and execution
Identify and implement process improvement and automation opportunities
Provide coaching and mentoring support to other Business Analysts within the team
Contribute to continuous improvement of business analysis standards, tools and ways of working
  
  
What will you bring?

Significant experience as a Business Analyst delivering complex initiatives
Strong experience working across Agile and Waterfall methodologies
Demonstrated strength in planning, organisation and delivery management
Proven executive-level stakeholder management capability
Experience working within banking regulated and compliance-driven environments
Solid experience with testing and quality assurance activities
Experience delivering process improvement or automation initiatives
High standard of written and verbal communication
Experience with CRM platforms such as Dynamics or Salesforce
Exposure to UX/UI concepts, including wireframing is desirable
IIBA or equivalent business analysis certification is desirable

With over $9 billion in assets and a head office in Perth and corporate offices in Coffs Harbour and Brisbane, we service over 200,000 customers across Australia from all walks of life supported by over 600 employees in group services, retail banking, broker, and business banking roles.
  
We believe our culture is a real point of difference, and our values – Focus on Members, Improve & Learn and Trust & Collaborate – are fundamental to our ways of working. We offer a collaborative and inclusive workplace where our people feel a strong sense of belonging, are recognised, invested in, cared for, and are part of something bigger.
  
What does our recruitment process look like?
The process can include a phone screen, virtual and/or face-to-face interview, psychometric testing, reference checks and all relevant background checks.
At P&N Group, we want to leverage the numerous benefits and innovative gains that a diverse workforce brings, and especially the high engagement that comes from a culture built on belonging and inclusion. We encourage people of all abilities, backgrounds, identities, ages and cultures to apply.

If you need assistance or require flexibility to be able to fully participate in the application process, please email us at recruitment@pnbank.com.au

JOB OVERVIEW:

We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support Biztrainer, a safety‑critical Learning and Contractor Management platform operating within a License to Operate landscape. This is a highly accountable role in a complex, integrated operational environment, where the quality of analysis and delivery directly impacts safety, compliance, and operational continuity.

This is not a standard BA role and requires strong operational engagement, sound judgement, and confidence working in high‑risk delivery contexts.


Key Responsibilities:

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis activities, including discovery, validation, and stakeholder playback
Facilitate and manage complex workshops with operational, safety, and technical stakeholders
Elicit, analyse, and document business, functional, and non‑functional requirements across integrated systems
Translate operational and compliance requirements into clear, actionable artefacts for delivery teams
Act as a trusted interface between business, operations, and technical teams
Leverage and adapt existing blueprints, patterns, and lessons learned from previous site implementations
Identify risks, gaps, and dependencies within tightly integrated operational workflows
Provide confident, constructive challenge where scope, assumptions, or delivery approaches may introduce operational risk

Scope & System Context

Biztrainer extends beyond a traditional LMS and includes:
Ongoing competency and learning compliance for operational site personnel
Contractor onboarding and contractor lifecycle management, a critical operational risk area
Deep integration with frontline operational workflows rather than back‑office processes
Interaction with safety‑critical and compliance‑heavy systems, including health and safety data

Required Experience & Capabilities

Senior‑level experience as a Business Analyst in complex enterprise environments
Strong stakeholder engagement capability, with proven credibility working directly with operational teams
Demonstrated experience running workshops end‑to‑end (discovery, validation, playback)
Ability to clearly translate between business needs and technical solutions
Experience in safety‑critical, operational, or compliance‑heavy industries (e.g. mining, oil & gas, heavy industry) highly regarded.
Solid understanding of integrated systems landscapes rather than standalone applications
Proactive, confident mindset with the ability to challenge constructively when required

Delivery Expectations

This role requires regular onsite presence (weekly or at a minimum monthly) to effectively engage with operational stakeholders
Not suitable for fully remote delivery
Operates in a high‑risk delivery environment with direct License to Operate implications, requiring maturity, decisiveness, and strong professional judgement

The Operations & AI role is a strategic, senior role responsible for transforming data into intelligence that drives operational excellence .

This role goes beyond traditional reporting: the Analytics Specialist will harness the power of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and advanced analytics to optimise critical services delivery, improve driver and network efficiency, strengthen customer reporting, and support evidence-based decision-making at every level of the business.

Working at the intersection of data science, this leader will build scalable analytics capabilities that directly enhance patient outcomes, reduce operational risk, and support ambitious growth across Australia.

Key Responsibilities:

Analytics & Reporting

Collect, analyse, interpret, and present data from across the business to enable high-quality operational and executive decision-making

Use the reporting tools to create visually immersive, interactive, and real-time dashboards

Prepare management reports that clearly articulate trends, patterns, and forward-looking predictions using relevant operational data

Tie together disparate and unrelated data sets – including driver GPS data, POD records, booking system data, temperature logs, and financial data – to produce unified, actionable insights

Perform continuous quality assurance on all data pipelines and reporting, detecting breaks, anomalies, and integrity issues and determining solutions

AI & Advanced Analytics

Design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning models to optimise operations

Build and refine AI-generated routing and dispatch logic that accounts for live traffic conditions, driver availability, and urgency classification to reduce delivery times and improve service reliability

Develop predictive analytics models

Leverage AI to identify operational risk patterns

Evaluate and implement AI tools, large language models (LLMs), and automation capabilities that enhance workflow efficiency

Champion the use of generative AI for internal productivity – including prompt engineering frameworks, AI-assisted report narration, and intelligent alerting systems

Operational Strategy & Stakeholder Engagement

Work alongside cross functional teams to establish current business intelligence needs and prioritise the analytics roadmap accordingly

Lead cross-state workforce planning initiatives, influencing and governing cross-functional leaders and teams through data-driven insights rather than direct authority

Translate complex analytical findings into clear, practical recommendations that allow operational stakeholders to take immediate, realistic action

Facilitate collaborative planning sessions with stakeholders, aligning subject matter expertise with organisational strategy and data capability

Build trust and credibility with cross-functional stakeholders by embedding data and AI tools into everyday decision-making workflows

Drive accountability and execution by guiding teams from insight to action, ensuring all analytics initiatives are understood, owned, and delivered 

Data Infrastructure & Governance

Make recommendations on data collection methods, system integrations, and analytics infrastructure to improve data quality, coverage, and analytical efficiency

Collaborate with IT to ensure data from all of our systems

Academic Qualifications

Tertiary qualifications, preferably in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, or a related analytical discipline, with a high level of academic achievement

Postgraduate study, certifications, or specialised training in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or Applied Data Science is highly regarded

Professional certifications in AI/ML platforms (e.g. Microsoft Azure AI, Google Cloud AI, AWS Machine Learning) or data visualisation tools are advantageous

Consulting Data Engineer

DyFlex Solutions is an established SAP Platinum Partner with offices across Australia and New Zealand. We are recognised as the local leader in SAP expertise, awarded many times as the best performing partner in multiple categories in the last few years. We deliver fit-for-purpose SAP solutions, with services delivering greenfield, upgrade and transformative implementations. We also deliver the project management, change management and ongoing support services to enable our staff to deliver excellence for our customers.

As the leader in SAP, Dyflex is now establishing a team to become the leader in enabling business centric AI & Data solutions. With SAP integrating Datasphere as an extension to Data Lakes such as Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery and Microsoft Fabric, we are seeking pragmatic thought leaders to help shape the shift of AI & Data engineering into the era of agentic/context engineering.

About the Role:
As a Consulting Data Engineer, you’ll design, build, and deploy scalable data pipeline and machine learning solutions that deliver real business value. You’ll use strong SQL and modern data stacks to create reliable, cost effective pipelines and support ML workloads. Experience with at least one of Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, MS Fabric is required, more than one a bonus. Experience with tools like Spark, dbt, Dataform is a plus but not required. You will work on meaningful technical challenges with autonomy and communicate technical outcomes clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

We value engineers who think creatively, communicate effectively, and engage confidently with stakeholders. We’re looking for engineers who do more than write code. You’ll listen to client challenges, dig into the core problem, help shape solutions, and explain them clearly. If you want to build something from the ground up with a team that’s already proven it can deliver meaningful outcomes, we’d like to hear from you.

Your tasks and responsibilities:

Build and maintain scalable data pipelines for ingesting, transforming, and delivering data
Manage and optimise databases, warehouses, and cloud storage solutions
Implement data quality frameworks and testing processes to ensure reliable systems
Design and deliver cloud-based solutions (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Take technical ownership of project components and lead small development teams
Engage directly with clients, translating business requirements into technical solutions
Champion best practices including version control, CI/CD, and infrastructure as code

Your qualifications and experience:

Hands-on data engineering experience in production environments for corporate, enterprise or public sector organisations
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL; experience with at least one additional language (e.g. Java, Typescript/Javascript)
Experience with modern frameworks such as Apache Spark, Airflow, dbt, Kafka, or Flink
Background in building ML pipelines, MLOps practices, or feature stores is highly valued
Proven expertise in relational databases, data modelling, and query optimisation
Experience with agentic coding tools such as Claude Code, Cortex in personal projects or commercial context, highly regarded
Demonstrated ability to solve complex technical problems independently
Excellent communication skills with ability to engage clients and stakeholders
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field

What we offer:

Work with SAP’s latest technologies on cloud as S/4HANA, BTP and Joule, plus Databricks, ML/AI tools and cloud platforms
A flexible and supportive work environment including work from home
Competitive remuneration and benefits including novated lease, birthday leave, salary packaging, wellbeing programme, additional purchased leave, and company-provided laptop
Comprehensive training budget and paid certifications (Databricks, SAP, cloud platforms, Snowflake, BigQuery)
Structured career advancement pathways with mentoring from senior engineers
Exposure to diverse industries and client environments

Join a renowned organisation delivering projects to some of Australia’s leading enterprises

DyFlex is committed to providing a safe, flexible and respectful environment for staff free from all forms of discrimination, bullying and harassment. We are proud of our diverse and inclusive team as only together we can continually improve ourselves and achieve best outcomes for our customers– we are the region's leading SAP Platinum Partner!

Please note that we can’t offer sponsorship for this role and we expect a clear statement about your legal rights in Australia, especially if you are applying from overseas.

Emesent is a world leader in autonomous drone technology, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Our solutions are transforming how mining, engineering, infrastructure, and public safety industries capture, understand, and act on spatial data. We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own and drive the next generation of our products.


As a Senior Product Manager, you will own the strategy and execution for one or more product lines within Emesent’s portfolio. You’ll work at the intersection of hardware, software, and autonomy defining what we build, why, and how we bring it to market. This is a high-autonomy role where you’ll set direction, make trade-offs, and drive outcomes. You’ll collaborate closely with engineering leads, commercial teams, and users to ensure our products deliver measurable value in complex, real-world environments.

What you’ll do

Product Strategy & Roadmap: Define the product strategy and roadmap for your area. Translate business objectives, market signals, and user needs into a clear, prioritised plan that balances near-term delivery with longer-term bets

Customer & Market Insight: Deeply understand our users workflows, pain points, and environments. Conduct field visits, user research, and competitive analysis to ensure product decisions are grounded in real-world evidence

Cross-Functional Leadership: Drive alignment across engineering, design, commercial, and client success teams. Drive sprint-level prioritisation within your squads and ensure the team is focused on the highest-impact work

Go-to-Market Execution: Partner with commercial and marketing teams to develop positioning, pricing, and launch strategies. Ensure users and internal teams are well- prepared for every release

Product Analytics: Define and monitor key product metrics. Use quantitative and qualitative data to inform decisions, measure outcomes, and continuously improve the user experience

Stakeholder Communication: Keep leadership and cross-functional stakeholders informed with clear, concise updates on progress, risks, and strategic decisions

What you’ll bring

5+ years of experience in product management, with demonstrated ownership of a product or product area end-to-end

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.

Proven ability to work effectively with engineering teams and translate technical complexity into clear product direction

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority across functions and seniority levels

Entrepreneurial mindset, you thrive in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and have a strong bias for action

Deep empathy for users and a passion for solving real-world problems in complex operating environments

Experience with Agile methodologies and tools such as Jira, Amplitude, or similar

Strongly regarded

Experience in the mining, geospatial, or survey industries

Track record of zero-to-one product development, taking a product from concept through to launch and market traction

Experience building or managing platform software products (APIs, SDKs, data pipelines, or developer-facing tools)

Experience with deep-tech or hardware-integrated products, where product decisions span physical and digital domains

The perks

9-day fortnight - every second Friday off

Employee Share Option Plan (ESOP) share in Emesent’s success

Work alongside a friendly and passionate team building cutting-edge technology

Genuine learning and development opportunities, including access to LinkedIn Learning

Employee Assistance Program

One full day of leave each year for your work anniversary

Novated Lease options

Who are we?

Emesent is a world leader in drone autonomy, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Founded in 2018 with deep roots in Australia’s globally recognised CSIRO, we’ve built a reputation for delivering high-quality spatial data capture across mining, infrastructure, survey, and construction industries. Our flagship product, Hovermap, is an autonomous mobile scanning platform that combines advanced collision avoidance and autonomous flight with LiDAR mapping to capture data in hazardous, GPS-denied, and hard-to-reach environments. Mountable on drones, vehicles, or carried as a backpack, Hovermap has set the standard for mobile mapping in underground mining and confined spaces. Our newest product, GX1, extends Emesent’s mapping technology into the AEC and survey markets. GX1 is a ground-based LiDAR scanner purpose-built for above-ground
environments delivering fast, accurate spatial capture for surveying and reality capture professionals.

With a rapidly growing global customer base and multiple product lines spanning hardware, desktop software, and cloud, Emesent is at an exciting inflection point  and we’re looking for people who want to help shape what comes next.


Why Emesent?
At Emesent, you’ll work on technology that is genuinely shaping the future of how industries capture and understand the physical world. We offer a dynamic, collaborative environment, competitive compensation, and real opportunities for professional growth. Join us in pushing the boundaries of robotics and spatial intelligence.

Job Title: Senior Vice President Finance - Group Functions & Process Excellence
Ad Text:
About Toll Group

At Toll, we do more than just logistics - we move the businesses that move the world. Our 16,000 team members can help solve any logistics, transport, or supply chain challenge – big or small. We have been supporting our customers for more than 130 years. Today, we support more than 20,000 customers worldwide with 500 sites in 27 markets, and a forwarding network spanning 150 countries. We are proudly part of Japan Post — www.tollgroup.com

The Senior Vice President Finance - Group Functions & Process Excellence is a strategic leadership role within the Finance Division, reporting directly to the Group CFO. This is a hybrid role, combining two critical mandates that together drive cost discipline and operational excellence across the organization: 

Finance Business Partner to Group Functions – specifically HR, IT, Legal & Compliance, and Enterprise Services – providing financial stewardship, cost management, and strategic insight to enable these functions to operate efficiently and within budget on a global scale.

Ownership of Finance processes globally, driving continuous improvement, standardization, and optimization to enhance the efficiency, accuracy, and effectiveness of Finance operations across all markets.

The SVP is expected to bring exceptional financial acumen, strong commercial acumen, deep analytics capabilities, and deep process expertise, coupled with the leadership gravitas to engage and influence senior stakeholders at the Group Function level and across geographies.

Role Responsibilities include:


Finance Business Partnering

Serve as the primary Finance partner to the global heads of group functions, providing proactive financial advice, cost analysis, and performance insights.

Lead annual budgeting, quarterly forecasting, monthly business review, and long-range financial planning for the four Group Functions, ensuring alignment with Group-wide financial targets.

Monitor, analyse, and challenge cost performance across Group Functions, identifying opportunities to improve cost efficiency without compromising operational effectiveness.

Provide clear and timely financial reporting to Group Function leaders and the Group CFO, translating complex financial data into actionable insights.

Partner with Group Function leaders on business cases, investment decisions, and resource allocation, ensuring robust financial evaluation and governance.

Drive accountability for cost management within Group Functions, establishing and maintaining appropriate financial controls and governance frameworks.

Support Group Function leaders in managing third-party vendor and contract costs, ensuring value for money and alignment with approved budgets.

Finance Process Ownership & Optimisation

Own and govern the end-to-end Finance processes globally, including but not limited to procure-to-pay, record-to-report, and financial planning & analysis processes.

Develop and implement a global Finance process optimisation roadmap, identifying inefficiencies and designing solutions that improve speed, accuracy, and scalability.

Establish and maintain global process standards, policies, and operating procedures, ensuring consistency and compliance across all markets.

Lead process transformation initiatives, leveraging technology, automation, and best practices to modernise Finance operations.

Work closely with the Finance technology and systems teams to ensure process design is effectively supported by Finance systems and tools.

Measure and track process performance through defined KPIs, driving continuous improvement and accountability across the Finance function.

Act as a centre of expertise on Finance process matters, providing guidance and support to regional and local Finance teams globally.

People Leadership

Lead, inspire, and develop a high-performing team of approximately 20 Finance professionals across business partnering and process ownership disciplines.

Set clear direction and performance expectations, foster a culture of accountability and continuous learning, and build strong succession pipelines.

Manage team resources effectively across both workstreams, and champion diversity, equity, and inclusion within the team.

What you bring:


Exceptional financial acumen with strong skills in planning, budgeting, forecasting, and cost management, underpinned by deep knowledge of financial controls, governance frameworks, and the ability to translate complex data into compelling insights for senior audiences.

Proven expertise in Finance process design, re-engineering, and optimization, with hands-on experience in ERP platforms (e.g. SAP), Finance automation tools, and leading cross-functional transformation programs.

Exceptional stakeholder management and influencing skills, with the gravitas to build trusted relationships and drive alignment across functions, cultures, and geographies without direct authority.

Strategic yet detail-oriented, with a strong analytical mindset, continuous improvement orientation, and the resilience to lead effectively through complexity, ambiguity, and change.

Results-driven, inclusive leader of high integrity — collaborative, globally minded, and deeply committed to building high-performing teams and maintaining the highest standards of financial governance.

Proven people leadership capability, with experience building, developing, and inspiring sizeable teams, fostering a culture of accountability, continuous learning, and inclusion across diverse and geographically dispersed groups.

Qualifications:


Bachelor or Master degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business Administration, or a related discipline

What moves you?
 

At Toll, you can help play a vital role in delivering what matters. From food, fuel, medicine and rescue services, we keep businesses and communities thriving. Every day brings change. We see that as an opportunity. To be curious. To ask the right questions. And build meaningful connections. Because finding new ways to solve problems is what we do. With a bold vision to expand our global reach, our 16,000+ people bring a passion for progress. We collaborate in friendly, caring teams, supported by approachable leaders who give us the autonomy to quickly make decisions with impact. Learn and grow with industry-leading training, alongside talented experts. Feel empowered to take on diverse challenges and new responsibilities to move you, our customers, and our world further.

Are you excited about this role but are concerned you don’t meet all the requirements? If you have similar skills and are willing to learn then we encourage you to apply anyway. We know that some people hesitate to apply for jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Toll, we value a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re interested in this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly then please talk to us – you may be just the right candidate for this or other roles we have coming up.

At Toll everyone is welcome including those of all ages, ethnicities, genders and abilities. 

To find out more about us visit www.careers.tollgroup.com

You must be entitled to work in Australia and be prepared to undertake pre-employment checks including a criminal history check and medical.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, home and digital solutions.

Optus.ai is our centralised AI, Analytics, Business Intelligence and Data Science function. Our purpose is to accelerate the transformation of Optus with AI. We design, build and operate AI-enabled products, agents and copilots that augment decisions, automate processes and improve experiences across the enterprise.

We're looking for an AI Product Manager to support the development and delivery of AI products within our Customer, Enabling, or Technology product portfolios.

What you'll do:

Reporting to the Senior Director of AI Product, you'll contribute to the end-to-end product lifecycle for a single product or squad, working within a defined product strategy and be accountable for:

Supporting product strategy execution and roadmap delivery for a single AI product or squad, aligned to Optus.ai objectives and business goals.
Contributing to product backlogs by writing clear user stories and acceptance criteria that translate business requirements into actionable development work.
Conducting user research initiatives including interviews, surveys, usability testing, and behavioural analysis to understand user needs and validate solutions.
Collaborating with cross-functional squads including engineering, data science, design, and business stakeholders through the product development lifecycle.
Defining and analysing key product metrics and KPIs to track product health, usage, and business impact, using insights to inform product decisions.
Participating in agile product development practices, including sprint planning, backlog refinement, and retrospectives to ensure high-quality delivery.
Building and maintaining stakeholder relationships, gathering feedback from diverse groups to inform product decisions and maintain alignment.

What you'll bring:

We're looking for someone who's excited by the challenge of building AI products that deliver real value and eager to grow their product craft.

Must-haves:

Demonstrated experience supporting product development from concept to launch.
Experience working with cross-functional teams and contributing to product backlogs, roadmaps, and success metrics.
Strong commitment to user-centred design with ability to balance user needs, business objectives, and technical constraints.
Proficiency in product analytics and using data insights to drive product decisions.
Understanding of AI/ML concepts and ability to work effectively with technical teams.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with ability to articulate product concepts clearly and build stakeholder relationships.
Analytical problem-solving skills with structured approach to breaking down challenges into actionable solutions.

Experience in telecommunications or highly regulated industries.
